name: How to resolve "rich result markup issues" in Search Console
description: Fix rich result markup errors and boost visibility in Search Console with this step-by-step guide.
main_title: How to resolve "rich result markup issues" in Search Console
richtext:
What are "rich result markup issues" in Search Console?

Rich result markup issues in Google Search Console are problems with the special code on your website that provides additional information to search engines. This code helps create attractive and informative search results, such as star ratings or product prices. However, if there are issues with this code, it can cause errors or inconsistencies. Google Search Console alerts you to these problems so you can fix them. By resolving these issues, you can improve your website's visibility and user engagement in search results.

How to resolve "rich result markup issues" in Google Search Console:

‍

  1. Log in to your Google Search Console account.

‍

  1. Choose the website property for which you want to address the rich result markup issues.

‍

  1. In the left-hand sidebar, click "Search results" and then choose "Search appearance". Now you can see the “rich results” from the list. 

‍

  1. On the Rich Results page, you'll see a list of pages with issues. Click on a specific issue to view the details. Or you can visit search.google.com/test/rich-results page and submit your page URL to detect any rich results issues. 

‍

  1. Review the specific errors or warnings provided by Google. These will highlight the problems with your markup code.

‍

  1. Identify the pages or sections of your website that need attention based on the reported issues.

‍

  1. Refer to Google's documentation on structured data and rich results to understand the correct markup code and guidelines.
  1. Update your website's markup code according to the guidelines. Make sure to fix any missing or incorrect markup, unsupported data types, or violations of Google's guidelines.
  1. After making the necessary changes, test your updated markup using Google's rich results testing tool. This tool allows you to see how your pages will appear in search results and helps identify any remaining issues.

‍

  1. Once you're confident that the issues have been resolved, go back to Google search console and click on the "Validate Fix" button next to the specific issue. This will prompt Google to reprocess the affected pages and verify if the issues have been resolved.

‍

  1. Monitor the rich results report in Google Search Console periodically to ensure that the issues remain resolved and that your rich results are displayed correctly.

‍

Remember, it's important to be patient, as it may take some time for Google to reprocess your pages and reflect the changes in search results. By following these steps and diligently addressing the rich result markup issues, you can enhance your website's appearance and visibility in search engine result pages.
